_note: The outlet credibility table: the layer that makes this desk GoCheckMyNews. Every source domain the desk cites maps to a coarse bias lane and factual-reporting grade, ATTRIBUTED to the public charts that publish them (AllSides media bias ratings; Media Bias/Fact Check factual grades; Ad Fontes Media Bias Chart). We license nothing and claim no ratings of our own: these are seed values transcribed from the public charts on 2026-07-19 at a deliberately coarse granularity, shown to readers WITH attribution so the judgment stays traceable to its source. VERIFY against the current published charts before launch and re-verify quarterly (chart ratings move). bias lanes: left, lean-left, center, lean-right, right, libertarian, official-record. factual grades: high, mostly-high, mixed. A domain absent from this table renders as 'unrated'.

attribution.line: Outlet ratings per the public AllSides and Media Bias/Fact Check charts, seeded 2026-07-19. Ratings belong to those organizations, not to this desk.

federalreserve.gov.note: Primary source: the institution's own record, not journalism.

supremecourt.gov.note: Primary source: the court's own record, not journalism.

justice.gov.note: Primary source: the department's own record, not journalism.

congress.gov.note: Primary source: the legislative record, not journalism.

wsj.com.note: News side; the opinion side leans right and the desk treats opinion pieces as opinion, not sourcing.

dowjones.io.note: WSJ feed infrastructure domain; same rating as wsj.com.

nationalreview.com.note: Opinion-forward publication; the desk uses its reporting, labels its commentary.
